what_to_measure: Probability of fill by limit price distance, queue position, and future P&L
why_it_matters: Adverse selection makes winners less likely to fill
trade_rule: Backtest only tradable fills or charge an execution penalty

what_to_measure: Bid-ask, brokerage, taxes, exchange fees, slippage, and leverage-driven turnover
why_it_matters: Costs can erase most gross edge
trade_rule: Costs must be inside the signal backtest and walk-forward validation

what_to_measure: Input data quality, model assumptions, calibration stability, and out-of-domain cases
why_it_matters: A good model is useful only where the assumptions are reasonable
trade_rule: Do not trade when inputs are stale or the model is outside its valid regime

what_to_measure: Smile shape, term structure, arbitrage violations, and missing strikes
why_it_matters: IV is contract-specific and bad surface data creates false signals
trade_rule: Clean and interpolate the surface before ranking cheap/rich contracts

what_to_measure: Jump loss, drawdown, margin call path, and capital required through shocks
why_it_matters: Short-vol P&L can look smooth until the one loss that forces liquidation
trade_rule: Size to survive spike days and use defined-risk structures when needed

what_to_measure: Reason for trade, regime, IV/RV gap, fill quality, costs, and post-trade result
why_it_matters: Psychology improves when decisions are observable and repeatable
trade_rule: Reduce size or paper trade when you cannot explain why a trade failed
